purpose: Central storage hub — defines Store interface, all domain models (Channel, Message, MessageSummary, SyncState), all repository interfaces, and the SQLite-backed Open() factory

entry_points: Open returns a fully migrated Store or error — never partial state
single db connection shared across all repos

config: migrations/*.sql files embedded at compile time
valid writable file path for dbPath

invariants: Migration 001_initial.sql also creates the migrations table inside the SQL file, but migrations.go independently creates it via createMigrationsTable before reading files — the IF NOT EXISTS in 001 prevents conflict
channels.telegram_id is UNIQUE NOT NULL here — relaxed to nullable with a partial unique index in migration 003
After this migration, checkpoint stores heterogeneous strings — Telegram uses numeric string message IDs, RSS will use ETags or dates
CREATE UNIQUE INDEX WHERE telegram_id IS NOT NULL — partial index ensures Telegram channels can't share a telegram_id, while NULL values (non-Telegram sources) are unrestricted
risks: No cascade delete of messages/sync_state is enforced at the repository layer — relies entirely on ON DELETE CASCADE defined in the schema
When Create is a no-op (duplicate), message.ID stays 0 — callers that immediately use message.ID after Create on a potentially duplicate message will silently operate on an invalid ID
Migrations are checked and applied sequentially without locking — concurrent Open() calls on the same database (e.g., two processes) could both see count=0 and race to apply the same migration
patterns: scanChannel accepts interface{ Scan(...any) error } — works for both *sql.Row and *sql.Rows, avoiding duplication between single-row and multi-row queries
GetByChannelAndDate and GetUnsummarized* both JOIN with messages table — MessageSummaryRepository queries span two tables, coupling it tightly to messages schema
//go:embed migrations/*.sql bakes SQL into the binary at compile time — no runtime filesystem dependency for schema
Repository interfaces (ChannelRepository, MessageRepository, etc.) are defined alongside domain models in this file — callers depend only on this file's types, not on concrete repo implementations
openTestStore creates a fresh DB in t.TempDir() — each test gets an isolated database with all migrations applied, no mocking
